A test case for the Math.pow function with string inputs in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the `Math.pow` method in JavaScript. It checks if `Math.pow` can correctly interpret string inputs with extra spaces and decimal points by asserting that `Math.pow("2   ", "2.0")` evaluates to `4`. This test ensures that the `Math.pow` function can handle and convert string inputs to numbers before performing the power operation. The script provides narrow functionality focused on validating a specific aspect of the `Math.pow` method's input handling.
